title: Renn Volz
content:
{{Top|leg}} {{Character |type= |image=[[File:RennVolz.jpg]] |name=Renn Volz |homeworld=[[Little Kessel]]"[[The Second Kessel Run]]" |birth= |death= |species=[[Human/Legends|Human]] {{C|[[Kesselian]]}} |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]] |pronouns=He/him |height= |mass= |hair=[[Color/Legends|Brown]] |feathers= |eyes=Brown |skin= |cyber= |families=new |parents=new |partners=new |siblings=new |children=[[Mira Volz]] |affiliation= |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|My invention was meant to stop suffering, not to create it!|Renn Volz|}} '''Renn Volz''' was a [[Human/Legends|human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Kesselian]] [[Professor/Legends|professor]] and inventor who, in [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]], developed the ''[[Ionic Ring Ship]]'', which could control a [[Planet/Legends|planet's]] weather. Although Volz wanted only to use his creation to ease the suffering of people living on unstable worlds, his ''Ionic Ring'' was swiftly commandeered by the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], who wanted to use it to devastate [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]]-held planets. Using Volz's daughter [[Mira Volz|Mira]] as a hostage, the Imperial [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Bzorn]] forced Volz to [[Devastation of Rion|devastate]] [[Rion]], before attempting to do the same with his [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of [[Little Kessel]]. However, an escaped Mira and a crew of Rebels helped foil Bzorn's plot, and Volz destroyed his own ''Ionic Ring'' to keep it out of Imperial hands. ==Biography== {{Quote|Your daughter is now my hostage, Professor Volz! An Imperial guarantee... that you will use your ship to turn Rebel planets into burnt-out wastelands!"
"Madness! I refuse!"
"You will destroy the Rebel planets... or you will never see your daughter again... alive!|Bzorn and Renn Volz|}} Originally a native of the [[Seswenna system/Legends|Seswenna system]], Renn Volz proved to be a promising enough academic prospect to secure a scholarship to the prestigious [[University of Byblos/Legends|University of Byblos]], eventually becoming a [[Professor/Legends|Professor]] and inventor himself. By the [[Imperial Era/Legends|Imperial Era]], he had settled on [[Little Kessel]], where he raised his daughter [[Mira Volz|Mira]], and worked on various cutting edge projects. Although secretly opposed to the policies of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], he walked the [[Politics/Legends|political]] line in order to receive funding from the Imperials and other corporate sources.''[[Rebellion Era Sourcebook]]'' Among Volz's creations were an experimental [[sub-space image transmitter]] and an experimental [[Starship/Legends|starship]] known as the ''[[Ionic Ring Ship]]'' which could control a [[Planet/Legends|planet's]] weather from above. Volz worked out of a hidden [[Volz Complex|laboratory]] on Little Kessel, hidden deep within the [[Giant Fungi Forest|Great Fungi Forest]]. By [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]], Volz's ''Ionic Ring'' had reached the point where it was ready to test, which he and his daughter did successfully by calming the chaotic weather of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Zerm]]. [[File:Mira_and_Renn_Volz.png|thumb|left|250px|Renn Volz with his daughter Mira.]] Volz had hardly finished his work on Zerm, however, when his ship was suddenly boarded by an [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]] party led by [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Bzorn]]. Bzorn, who had been personally sent by [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] to hunt down and destroy [[Alliance to Restore the Republic|Rebel]]-held worlds. Bzorn forcibly commandeered the ''Ionic Ring'', kidnapping Mira to ensure his compliance. Bzorn's first target was the planet [[Rion]]—after the inhabitants of the world refused to give up the Rebel base, Bzorn forced Volz to use the ''Ionic Ring'' to [[Devastation of Rion|turn it into a frozen wasteland]]. Little Kessel was the next planet on Bzorn's list, and despite his distress, Volz afflicted his homeworld with [[Groundquake/Legends|earthquakes]] and cyclones of devastating intensity. However, Volz soon reached his breaking point, and refused to continue the assault on his homeworld, accepting whatever consequences might come. What Volz didn't know was that Bzorn had been bluffing all along: Mira had slipped away from Imperial custody before Rion, joining a crew of Rebels and making their way to Little Kessel, There, she used Volz's sub-space image transmitter to beam a message into the ''Ionic Ring'' and inform her father that she was safe. Overjoyed, Volz then watched as Mira's Rebel friends—[[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]], [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] and [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]]—stormed the ''Ionic Ring''{{'s}} control room, starting a firefight that killed Bzorn. Fearing that the Empire could still use the ''Ionic Ring'' for ill, Volz set the ship to self-destruct, before narrowly fleeing his creation and happily reuniting with his daughter on Little Kessel. ==Personality and traits== {{Quote|No! No... my daughter means everything to me! I'll... do your bidding!|Renn Volz, to Bzorn|}} An innovative scientist, Volz believed that all beings should benefit from scientific advancement, leading him to hate what he saw as the divisive policies of the Empire. However, Volz was able to keep those feelings personal, and publicly express enough support for the Empire to receive funding for his projects. Volz wanted only to use his creations to help the people of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]], and was aghast at the idea that they would be used for violent ends. However, Volz felt that his daughter Mira was the most important thing in his life, and his desire to protect her was stronger than his desire to protect innocent lives. Even that went only so far, however, as he was willing to submit to whatever Captain Bzorn had in store for him and his daughter in order to stop the attack on Little Kessel. ==Behind the scenes== Renn Volz first appeared in ''[[The Second Kessel Run]]'', a [[Star Wars (comic strip)|comic strip]] story arc written by [[Russ Helm]] and released in [[1980]]. In the comic, Volz was illustrated by [[Russ Manning]]. Volz was later featured in the [[2001]] ''[[Star Wars Roleplaying Game]]'' supplement ''[[Rebellion Era Sourcebook]]'', which provides an account for his life that directly contrasts with ''The Second Kessel Run''. The ''Rebellion Era Sourcebook'' portrays Volz as having been conscripted by [[Grand Moff/Legends|Grand Moff]] [[Wilhuff Tarkin/Legends|Tarkin]] and pressed into Imperial service at the [[Maw Installation]], where he would create the ''Ionic Ring''. According to the sourcebook, Volz, sequestered at [[Maw Cluster/Legends|the Maw]], had no idea that the Empire was actually using his inventions for violent purposes. ==Appearances== *"[[The Second Kessel Run]]" {{Reprint|The Second Kessel Run}} {{1st}} ==Sources== *{{CollectorCite|3|ComicScan|ComicScan: Coming Full Circle}} *''[[Rebellion Era Sourcebook]]'' *''[[Star Wars: The Comics Companion]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of Renn Volz}} {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |nl=Renn Volz }} {{DEFAULTSORT:Volz, Renn}} [[Category:Engineers]] [[Category:Humans]] [[Category:Individuals with he/him pronouns]] [[Category:Kesselians]] [[Category:Males]]
